facts.text: San Jose was founded on November 29, 1777, as the Pueblo de San José de Guadalupe, the first civilian settlement in California.
The city’s name comes from the Spanish “el Pueblo de San José de Guadalupe,” meaning the Town of Saint Joseph of Guadalupe.
Kelley Park hosts the historic Japanese Friendship Garden, History Park, and the Portuguese Historical Museum, all within a single green space.
San Jose’s San Jose Museum of Quilts & Textiles is the first museum in America dedicated solely to quilts and textiles as an art form.
The city’s historic streetcars, preserved at History Park, run on downtown light rail lines during holidays, echoing early 20th‑century transit.
San Jose’s PayPal Park will host eight Olympic soccer matches during the 2028 Summer Olympics, marking the city’s first Olympic venue.
